segments.text: Crossview. A cross-referenced MITRE security knowledge silo, and a five-stage code scanner, in one tool.

segments.caption: crossview show CWE-89
segments.text: The whole MITRE graph lives in one local database. Ask about any entity, like CWE-89, SQL injection, and Crossview shows it with every cross-reference, in both directions.

segments.caption: crossview search
segments.text: Full-text search spans the entire silo: weaknesses, attack patterns, ATT&CK, ATLAS, D3FEND, and the Unified Kill Chain.

segments.caption: crossview dev stats
segments.text: That is roughly thirty-seven hundred canonical entities and fourteen thousand cross-references, all offline.

segments.caption: crossview tui
segments.text: Browse it interactively in the terminal: tree views, search, and a detail panel for any entity.

segments.caption: crossview graphql
segments.text: Or query it programmatically. The built-in GraphQL surface walks a weakness straight through its exploit chain, from CWE to CAPEC to ATT&CK to D3FEND.

segments.caption: crossview scan
segments.text: Point Crossview at a codebase, and its five-stage scanner threads every finding through that same graph, then intersects it with the CVEs that are actually being exploited in the wild.
Crossview. Know the weakness, the attack, and the defense, before you ship.
